name: HillaryClinton.com
subject: How Donald Trump gets to the White House
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a campaign supported by hundreds of thousands of grassroots donors
body:
Just the facts | Friend --

Here are some facts:

1. Donald Trump could become the presumptive Republican nominee if he wins Ohio and Florida this Tuesday.

2. If that happens, the only thing standing between Donald Trump and the presidency will be the Democratic nominee -- and that person is going to need all the time they can get to focus on stopping Trump.

3. There is one person, and one person only, who we can trust to be the last line of defense against Donald Trump succeeding Barack Obama as the 45th President of the United States: Hillary Clinton.

We need to make sure Hillary is the nominee to take him on -- and to get there, we’ve got to win the big big big primaries on Tuesday. **That’s going to be hard to do if our opponent continues to outspend us, so before Tuesday, chip in, and let's go build a winning campaign:**

name: Doug Stafford
subject: Check this out, Patriot
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rand Paul for Senate
body:
Patriot,
I'm not sure if you saw this message from
Rand yesterday, but this is serious.
With so much uncertainty in Washington, it's
clear we need a clear voice for liberty in the Senate, now more
than ever.
But we need your help to make sure Rand can
get back to DC and ensure that our courts are doing the job
they're supposed to.
Will you check out the important message
below and help us out by contributing just $25 today?
Your support is always appreciated.

In Liberty,
Doug Stafford
---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Rand Paul
Subject: Need your support now
To: Patriot <dpwillis67@gmail.com>
Dear Patriot,

A win for me in November is a win for the
country. Let me tell you why:
President Obama thinks he has the chance to
cement liberal control of the U.S. Supreme Court for decades --
and only a Republican Senate can stop him.

HELP ME STOP HIM NOW
>>

If Democrats win the Senate and lose the
White House in November, they have the option to change the
entire path of our nation by forcing through a liberal
replacement to Justice Scalia's seat.
That's because a new Congress begins January
3rd, 2017, and President Obama will be still in Office until
January 20, 2017.
That means they have two weeks for the
President to send a nominee and with a Democrat majority, the
Senate could apply the 'nuclear option' and vote in the nominee
with only 51 votes.
This would be a complete nightmare. I need
your support now to make sure it doesn't happen.
I cannot stress this enough: losing the
Senate in November is not an option.
I do not believe a GOP majority should
confirm a Supreme Court nomination from President Obama and this
has made it more clear than ever that the role of the Senate is
vital to maintaining liberty.
Patriot, I need your help to win another
term, to stand up for common sense in Washington.
Without a strong Senate, we cannot ensure
that our courts are upholding the Constitution.
That's why I am more committed to another
term for Kentucky and for this country.
Please stand with me today.

In Liberty,
Rand Paul

Pitch in $25 TODAY >>

Pitch in $50 TODAY >>

Pitch in $75 TODAY >>

Pitch in $100 TODAY
>>

Or donate another
amount >>

Paid for by Rand Paul for Senate

| | | | Peter, you've probably heard what happened in Chicago last night: Donald Trump had to cancel a rally after a large group of protesters gathered and violence broke out. The images from the event look like something we would see in a third-world country, not the greatest nation on earth. It's a sad day in America. This morning, Marco held a press conference where he said if this continues, our nation will be ripped apart at the seams and we will be incapable of solving any of the major issues that we have. There's a broader issue in our political culture in this country, and this is what happens when a leading presidential candidate like Donald Trump goes around feeding into a narrative of anger and bitterness and frustration.

body:
www.JoeSestak.com
CUP OF JOE

March 12th, 2016
Newsletter

Good morning! I wanted to provide an update about what I've been up to and where I've traveled. Thanks so much for reading, I truly appreciate your support!

Speaking at State Committee

As President John F. Kennedy said so well, "the hour is late, but the agenda is long." At State Committee last weekend, I explained how in the last 6 years that agenda has grown longer. I want to begin on that agenda for people above anything else -- people above party and type. Click the video below to watch the speech!

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jBpwpOhyhs0

Up in the Polls

We are now up by 16 points on our closest primary opponent -- before airing a single TV ad.

These are good poll numbers, but here
are some other numbers explaining why:
• 20,000 grassroots volunteers
• 500,000 brochures handed out in 2015 alone
• 8,000 brochures handed out daily in 2016 (remaining boxes to the right!)
• 12,000 ballot signatures collected
• 7 stops on our 2-day tour of the state on PA Working families in January
• 7 more stops on our 2-day statewide tour in February on national security
• Over 60 events in between, with an average of 2 radio shows per day

The Daily Courier
Guest Commentary
By JOE SESTAK
A new national security strategy for the 21st century

For the first time since 2006, terrorism tops the economy as our number one concern, and eight in ten Americans believe that another attack is at least somewhat likely. We have not been this worried about terrorism since September 11.

And yet, terrorism is only one of many challenges on the spectrum of security threats we must address.

In the last year, we have had to deal with an increasingly aggressive China. A belligerent Russia. The menace of infectious diseases. Reckless moves by North Korea. Cyberattacks. Climate change.

Among those currently seeking the public's trust to lead our nation, few have articulated a coherent national security framework that explains how we should understand and tackle these global challenges.

This is troubling.

We urgently need a smart national security strategy to deal with such a diversity and range of challenges. By "smart," I mean we have to frame a strategy that will empower us to deal with various challenges with equal effectiveness - from Russia to China to ISIS to North Korea.

There are four pillars around which we should frame a national security strategy that explains and suits the needs of our time.

First, diplomacy matters - and there is no way around it. Our recent efforts to rein in Iran's nuclear ambition is a perfect example. Sure, our military could have launched strikes against their reactors, but that would delay a nuclear weapon for only a few years.

Instead, painstaking diplomacy - under American leadership - has pressured Iran to ship about 25,000 pounds of nuclear materials to Russia, meaning that, for the first time in a decade, Iran no longer has enough low-enriched uranium needed to make a nuclear bomb.

As a former military man, I want this negotiated deal to succeed. The more effective our diplomacy, the less likely our brave men and women would be put into harm's way. Iraq should have taught us that we should never use force unless we have an exit strategy.

Second, in a global world, no nation stands alone. This will be particularly apparent in the coming months as we work closely with our global allies to share intelligence, support police actions against terror suspects, run bombing raids, and conduct joint on-the-ground operations to capture and kill ISIS members in Syria and beyond.

I saw this first hand after 9/11, when I commanded a battle group in the Persian Gulf. I had 30 ships to carry out my mission - but only 10 of them were American ships. We were a coalition, and success was defined by my ability to work seamlessly with foreign militaries.

Third, economic security is national security - we cannot have one without the other.

Today, China is illegally claiming control over most of the South China Sea to secure the undersea resources that it needs for continued economic development. In the process, they are disrupting the entire region.

The South China Sea may be over 7,000 miles away from our shores. But remember that $5 trillion worth of goods flow through that sea every year, about one-fifth of which involve the United States. We need that region to remain stable for American-made goods designed and produced by American workers to reach Asia's growing consumer class.

We simply cannot meet our goal of boosting American exports - and creating good jobs here at home - when we cannot trade in peace.

For our long-term security and economic prosperity, staying engaged in the world is a must. This is especially true in Asia - home to some of the world's most vibrant economies of this century. China's behavior, whether unilateral moves in the seas or hacking companies in cyberspace, will directly impact every country's national interests.

That's why the United States must remain a robust Pacific nation, keeping pressure on China to play by the rules.

Finally, we have to stop measuring military power by numbers of ships or brigades, and learn how to rapidly acquire the knowledge to win. In a world dictated by speed, we should invest in the newest technology to gather critical intelligence and, with that, carry out swift action - from striking terrorist cells to shielding Seoul from North Korean aggression.

We need to focus on force posture, not structure. It makes no sense to buy submarines at $2 billion each when we can invest in high-tech netted sensor information system to track enemy underwater movements, and then direct an aircraft to drop a torpedo for the "kill."

This works whether we are up against a nation-state or a loose terrorist cell - the flexibility that we need to confront today's diverse threats.

None of this will be easy, as there is no set answer to our challenges. But in a world where our solutions must come in different shapes and sizes - sometimes a combination of our available resources - smarts and agility may just be our best currencies.

Sitting Down with AL DÍA

I had a great discussion yesterday wi th AL DÍA' s editorial staff, which you can read about in an upcoming edition. As enjoyable was my discussion in the lobby with a fellow veteran, Sgt. Jose Melendez, who keeps staff and building visitors safe. It just goes to show how diversity in our services so enriches us.

Thanks for reading -- and please click the "contribute" button to keep our momentum going.

Joe

Facebook: www.facebook.com/joesestak
Twitter: twitter.com/JoeSestak
www.joesestak.com

P.O. Box 1936
Media, PA 19063

> Katherine

| Peter -- Go Nats!! Tonight Marco won the DC primary thanks to thousands of conservatives like you who stood in line for hours to vote for Marco. We've been saying for a while now that it's going to be a delegate fight, and Marco just picked up a bunch this Saturday: His win in the DC primary is expected to get Marco 10 delegates -- and Donald Trump gets none! The way we got that win is even more important: It took real dedication and organizing hustle to get people to the polls on a Saturday, and then to wait for _**hours**_ in order to vote. I know: I personally stood in line for 3.5 hours to vote Marco. Marco's doing his part in Florida, and on Saturday, conservatives here in DC did theirs -- so thank you. It was an incredibly tight race, and every single Marco supporter helped us win it. But this fight is FAR from over. As we've said, the single biggest delegate prize of 2016 is this Tuesday, in Florida, and it's going to be a tight race, too -- just with a LOT more on the line.
So there's two things you can do right now: [**Chip in $10 to celebrate Marco's DC victory!**](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/46592586:6ft3i0rN4:m:1:1349778030:929D04F02DFB97D44746CB78A2A49F97:r) [**And go here to sign up for our call-from-home app.**](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/46592587:6ft3i0rN4:m:1:1349778030:929D04F02DFB97D44746CB78A2A49F97:r) We need as many conservatives as possibly making calls to Florida these final days, telling people why they believe in Marco and why we need to support him to stop Trump in Florida. A vote in DC helped, but a couple hours on the phone to Florida will be worth even more! Thanks again -- and get excited for the home stretch into Florida. name: Bernie Sanders 2016
subject: We've come too far to come up short
body:
#
Sisters and Brothers -
In just about 72 hours, we'll be watching the results come in from North Carolina, Florida, Illinois, Ohio, and Missouri.
We've come so far -- too far to come up short now.
Because when we win against the most inevitable candidate in decades, every single campaign after ours will be forced to think twice before accepting big checks from Wall Street, the pharmaceutical industry, and people who profit from destroying our environment.
And when that happens, we can start to break free of a corrupt political system that results in Wall Street regulating Washington, D.C., and not the other way around.
Those are the stakes on Tuesday night and in this primary. And I know that if we stand together, we are going to do very, very well.
[**Contribute $3 to our campaign today and we 're gonna win the delegates we need to win on Tuesday night, we're gonna win this primary, and then the White House in November.**](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/tuesdays-contests?refcode=em160312full "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/tuesdays-contests?refcode=em160312full")
We are closer in a number of the states voting on Tuesday than we were at this point before our historic victory in Michigan. But time is running out for you to contribute to help put us over the top.
That's why your donation matters so much, and why I have to ask you to make one before it's too late.
In solidarity,
Jeff Weaver
Campaign Manager
Bernie 2016
**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/tuesdays-contests?refcode=em160312full "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/tuesdays-contests?refcode=em160312full")**
